description: Fix timeout error in gcloud-api being classified as internal (non-retryable) instead of timeout (retryable).
qualityChecks: cd plugins/google-cloud && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Classify timeout rejection as ToolError.timeout instead of ToolError.internal
userStories.description: The GAPI request wrapper creates a timeout rejection with status 0 that falls through to ToolError.internal in classifyGapiError. Detect status 0 and throw ToolError.timeout directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeout errors are classified as ToolError.timeout with retryable=true
The error message mentions the timeout duration
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/google-cloud/src/gcloud-api.ts (fix timeout classification)

Context:
The GAPI wrapper (around line 150) creates a timeout rejection:
```typescript
reject({ status: 0, result: { error: { code: 0, message: 'Request timed out after 25 seconds' } } });
```

This goes to `classifyGapiError` which has a `default` case that throws `ToolError.internal()`. Status 0 with code 0 hits this default.

Fix: Add a case for status 0 / code 0 in `classifyGapiError` that throws `ToolError.timeout()`, OR detect the timeout before calling `classifyGapiError` and throw `ToolError.timeout()` directly.

Read the `classifyGapiError` function to find the right place to add the case.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the timeout duration
- Do NOT change other error classifications
